name: Margubete, Ezcaray – circular por Collado de Sagastia
region_intro: Mountain biking around Santo Domingo De La Calzada offers diverse terrain with significant elevation changes, catering to various skill levels. The region features a mix of mountains, valleys, and extensive forest tracks, including notable beech forests that provide scenic backdrops. Natural water elements like the Grajera Reservoir and the Oja River are integrated into many routes, enhancing the riding experience. This area provides a range of challenging ascents and rewarding descents. Best mountain bike trails around Santo Domingo De La Calzada The most popular mountain bike trail is La Grajera Reservoir – San Antón Hill loop from Santo Domingo de la Calzada, a 54.3 miles (87.4 km) trail that takes 6 hours 20 minutes to complete. This difficult route features varied terrain and significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Ascent to Santa Bárbara – Collado de Sagastia loop from Santo Domingo de la Calzada, a difficult 55.2 miles (88.9 km) path. This route demands good fitness and offers extensive views across the landscape. Local mountain bikers also love the Santo Domingo de la Calzada, Ezcaray – circular por Villar de Torre, a 29.5 miles (47.5 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 8 minutes. Mountain biking around Santo Domingo De La Calzada is defined by varied terrain, extensive forests, and significant elevation changes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging routes. The routes in Santo Domingo De La Calzada are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Santo Domingo De La Calzada's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Santo Domingo De La Calzada?
A: There are over 35 mountain bike trails around Santo Domingo De La Calzada, offering a diverse range of experiences for different skill levels. You'll find everything from moderate rides to challenging routes with significant elevation changes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Santo Domingo De La Calzada?
A: The terrain around Santo Domingo De La Calzada is quite varied, featuring mountains, valleys, and extensive forest tracks. You can expect significant elevation changes on many routes, with some offering over 3,000 feet of elevation gain. The region also incorporates natural water elements like the Grajera Reservoir and the Oja River into its routes.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or those seeking an easier ride?
A: Yes, while many routes are challenging, there are 4 easy mountain bike trails available around Santo Domingo De La Calzada. These routes offer a gentler introduction to the region's beautiful landscapes.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993747314/margubete-ezcaray-circular-por-collado-de-sagastia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Margubete, Ezcaray – circular por Collado de Sagastia&lt;/a&gt; offer nearly 1,000 meters of elevation gain over 43 kilometers. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31876572" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Santa Bárbara – Collado de Sagastia loop from Santo Domingo de la Calzada&lt;/a&gt;, which features over 2,000 meters of ascent.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes around Santo Domingo De La Calzada?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in the area are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993747544/santo-domingo-de-la-calzada-ezcaray-circular-por-villar-de-torre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santo Domingo de la Calzada, Ezcaray – circular por Villar de Torre&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular route that takes you through varied landscapes.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Santo Domingo De La Calzada?
A: Autumn is particularly recommended for mountain biking in Santo Domingo De La Calzada, especially if you want to experience the vibrant colors of the region's beech forests. The pleasant weather during this season also makes for enjoyable rides.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in the area?
A: While many routes feature significant elevation, some sections of trails like the La Grajera Reservoir – San Antón Hill loop can be suitable for all skill levels, offering options for different durations. For easier rides, look for the 4 trails rated as 'easy' in the region.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: You can encounter several natural attractions and landmarks. Riders can enjoy views of the Grajera Reservoir and the Oja River, including a waterfall at the Mirador del Río Oja. Other notable features include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4705206" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;rock formations on the banks of the Tirón River&lt;/a&gt; and the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5550560" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Horse Chestnut Trees of Ezcaray&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites or castles near the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. You might encounter historical sites such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5557168" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Leiva Castle&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4705240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de los Velasco&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5338536" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Velasco Castle and Cuzcurrita de Río Tirón Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, adding a cultural dimension to your ride.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Santo Domingo De La Calzada?
A: The mountain bike trails around Santo Domingo De La Calzada are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 40 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, extensive forests, and the rewarding challenge of the significant elevation changes.

Q: How long do mountain bike trails typically take in Santo Domingo De La Calzada?
A: Trail durations vary significantly based on distance and difficulty. For instance, a moderate route like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993872341/santo-domingo-de-la-calzada-granon-circular-por-cuzcurrita-del-rio-tiron" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santo Domingo de la Calzada, Grañón – circular por Cuzcurrita del Río Tirón&lt;/a&gt;, covering about 37 km, can take around 3 hours. More challenging and longer routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10470877" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Grajera Reservoir – San Antón Hill loop&lt;/a&gt; (87 km), can take over 6 hours to complete.
tips:
In addition to the fact that it is the only church-fortress in La Rioja, what is most striking is that inside it houses a henhouse in which a real rooster and hen live, which are also protected as an Asset of Cultural Interest by the Government of La Rioja.
The animals are replaced every two weeks and allude to the legend of the hanged man. The myth tells that a pilgrim was hanged after being unjustly accused of theft. When his parents went to see the body they found their son alive and he told them that Santo Domingo de la Calzada had saved his life. The parents proclaimed that their son was alive, but the mayor of the city did not believe them and told them something like: "Your son is as alive as the roasted rooster and hen that I am eating." And suddenly, according to legend, the animals regained their feathers and came back to life.
Crossroads of paths, confluence of the GR 190, the Bonicaparra track, the Tres Aguas path.... Meadows usually colonized by cattle.

Open shelter, with easy access, in the Bonicaparra area, a former village of which no remains remain. The shelter is quite neglected, but at least it can serve as shelter. Fountain, grills and tables complete the recreational area.

As of June 2024, reconditioned and improved. Let's see if we can keep it like this, please..!!!!
